title: Suspicious network traffic identification method and apparatus
abstract: Apparatuses, methods and storage medium associated with techniques to identify suspicious network connections. In embodiments, an apparatus may include an analysis function to be operated by the one or more processors to receive a collection of network data records, and apply a latent factor model to the network data records to identify a subset of the network data records as suspicious network connections. Other embodiments may be disclosed or claimed.
claims:
Claims Claims What is claimed is: 1. An apparatus for identifying suspicious network connections, comprising: one or more processor; and an analysis function to be operated by the one or more processors to receive a collection of network data records, and apply a latent factor model to the data to identify a subset of the network data records as suspicious network connections. 2. The apparatus of claim 1, wherein the analysis function is further to train the latent factor model. 3. The apparatus of claim 2, wherein to train the latent factor model, the analysis function is to identify connection types, and bin the network data records by connection types. 4. The apparatus of claim 1, wherein to identify a subset of the network data records as suspicious network connections, the analysis function is further to use the latent factor model to generate a probabilistic model for network traffic and assign a likelihood score to each connection indicating a likelihood of a particular device sending or receiving a given connection from/to another device on the network 5. The apparatus of claim 4, wherein to generate the probabilistic model, the analysis function is to form a sparse matrix of connections counts by type and device, based at least in part on a count of the number of times each type of connection is observed, for each IP address, in a given time period. 6. The apparatus of claim 5, wherein the analysis function is to further factor the sparse matrix into a d x k device matrix, F, and a k x w connections matrix, W, where d is the number of devices being analyzed, w the number of connection types and k is the number of latent factors being used. 7. The apparatus of claim 6, wherein the analysis function is to further compute a plurality of probabilities, one for each device, being involved in a particular connection as: where is a 1xk vector of factor weights for device i, is a kx1 vector of factor weights for connection type k and and are biases for device i and connection type k respectively. 8. The apparatus of any one of claims 1-7, wherein the analysis function is further 

 output the identified suspicious network connections in a graphical user interface (GUI) for analysis by an analyst. 9. The apparatus of claim 8, wherein the analysis function is to further filter or sort the identified suspicious network connections, in response to instructions from the analyst via the GUI. 10. The apparatus of claim 8, wherein the analysis function is to further train the latent factor model based on feedback from the analyst. 11. A method for identifying suspicious network connections, comprising: receiving, by a computing device, a collection of network data records; and applying, by the computing device, a latent factor model to the data to identify a subset of the network data records as suspicious network connections. 12. The method of claim 11, further comprising training the latent factor model, wherein training the latent factor model comprises identifying connection types, and binning the network data records by connection types. 13. The method of claim 11, wherein applying comprises generating, using the latent factor model, a probabilistic model for network traffic and assigning a likelihood score to each connection indicating a likelihood of a particular device sending or receiving a given connection from/to another device on the network, 14. The method of claim 13, wherein generating the probabilistic model comprises forming a sparse matrix of connections counts by type and device, based at least in part on a count of the number of times each type of connection is observed, for each IP address, in a given time period; factoring the sparse matrix into a d x k device matrix, F, and a k x w connections matrix, W, where d is the number of devices being analyzed, w the number of connection types and k is the number of latent factors being used; and computing a plurality of probabilities, one for each device, being involved in a particular connection as: where is a 1xk vector of factor weights for device i, is a kx1 vector of factor weights for connection type k and and are biases for device i and connection type k respectively. 15. The method of claim 11, further comprising outputting, by the computing device, the identified suspicious network connections in a graphical user interface (GUI) for analysis by an analyst. 16. The method of claim 15, further comprising filtering or sorting, by the computing 

 device, the identified suspicious network connections, in response to instructions from the analyst via the GUI. 17. The method of claim 15, further comprising training the latent factor model based on feedback from the analyst. 18. One or more computer-readable storage medium (CRM) having instructions to cause a computing device, in response to execution of the instructions by the computing device, to perform any one of the methods of claims 11-17. 19. An apparatus for identifying suspicious network connections, comprising: means for receiving a collection of network data records; and means for applying a latent factor model to the data to identify a subset of the network data records as suspicious network connections. 20. The apparatus of claim 19, further comprising means for training the latent factor model; wherein means for training the latent factor model comprises means for identifying connection types, and binning the network data records by connection types. 21. The apparatus of claim 19, wherein means for applying comprises means for using the latent factor model to generate a probabilistic model for network traffic and means for assigning a likelihood score to each connection indicating a likelihood of a particular device sending or receiving a given connection from/to another device on the network 22 The apparatus of claim 21, wherein means for using the latent factor model to generate the probabilistic model comprises means for forming a sparse matrix of connections counts by type and device, based at least in part on a count of the number of times each type of connection is observed, for each IP address, in a given time period; means for factoring the sparse matrix into a d x k device matrix, F, and a k x w connections matrix, W, where d is the number of devices being analyzed, w the number of connection types and k is the number of latent factors being used; and means for computing a plurality of probabilities, one for each device, being involved in a particular connection as: where is a 1xk vector of factor weights for device i, is a kx1 vector of factor weights for connection type k and and are biases for device i and connection type k respectively. 23. The apparatus of any one of claims 19-22, further comprising means for outputting the identified suspicious network connections in a graphical user interface 

 (GUI) for analysis by an analyst. 24. The apparatus of claim 23, further comprising means for filtering or sorting the identified suspicious network connections, in response to instructions from the analyst via the GUI. 25. The apparatus of claim 23, further comprising means for training the latent factor model based on feedback from the analyst.
